info.title: Kaleidoswap RGB-LSP API
info.description:
Public Maker API for KaleidoSwap market data, quotes, swap orders, and RGB LSPS1
channel operations.

Public Maker endpoints are unauthenticated today. Admin and wallet-management
routes remain private and are intentionally excluded from this contract.

Use the per-order `access_token` returned by LSPS1 and swap-order creation flows
when polling protected order status endpoints or submitting a rate decision after
the maker requests client confirmation.

get.description: Return the maker's RGB LSPS1 capabilities, supported assets, and channel limits. Safe to poll and cache.

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

503.description: The backing LSP node is unavailable.

get.description: Return the maker's current blockchain height and network. Safe to poll and cache.

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

503.description: The backing LSP node is unavailable.

post.description: Create an LSPS1 channel order. When the client is purchasing assets, a fresh `rfq_id` must be supplied and is extended to remain valid through the payment window. The response includes a per-order `access_token` for later polling or rate decisions. Treat this call as non-idempotent.

asset_purchase.summary: Create LSPS1 order with asset purchase

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

400.description: The LSPS1 parameters, quote, or access constraints are invalid.

example.message: RFQ ID is required when purchasing assets (client_asset_amount > 0). Please request a quote first.

details.message: RFQ ID is required when purchasing assets (client_asset_amount > 0). Please request a quote first.

404.description: The referenced asset was not found.

422.description: Request validation failed before reaching application logic.

503.description: The backing LSP node or required service is unavailable.

post.description: Estimate LSPS1 channel fees without creating an order. When the client is purchasing assets, a fresh `rfq_id` is required so the asset price can be folded into the total fee calculation.

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

400.description: The LSPS1 parameters or quote reference are invalid.

example.message: RFQ ID is required when purchasing assets (client_asset_amount > 0). Please request a quote first.

details.message: RFQ ID is required when purchasing assets (client_asset_amount > 0). Please request a quote first.

422.description: Request validation failed before reaching application logic.

503.description: The backing LSP node or required service is unavailable.

post.description: Fetch the current LSPS1 order state. Clients must provide the per-order `access_token` returned at creation time. This endpoint is safe to poll.

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

400.description: The supplied access token is invalid.

404.description: No LSPS1 order exists for the supplied order ID.

422.description: Request validation failed before reaching application logic.

post.description: Resolve an LSPS1 repricing event after the maker has moved the order into `PENDING_RATE_DECISION`. Clients must supply the per-order `access_token`. Accepting a new rate resumes the order with fresh pricing; declining initiates refund handling.

example.message: Rate accepted. Order will proceed with current market rate.

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

400.description: The order is not awaiting a rate decision or the access token is invalid.

example.message: Order is not pending rate decision. Current state: COMPLETED

404.description: No LSPS1 order exists for the supplied order ID.

422.description: Request validation failed before reaching application logic.

503.description: Fresh market pricing is currently unavailable.

example.message: Current market price unavailable. Please try again.

post.description: Request an immediate retry for LSPS1 asset delivery after a client returns online. This bypasses the normal backoff schedule and queues the order for priority processing. Repeating the call is safe but does not guarantee delivery success.

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

400.description: The supplied order ID is invalid.

example.message: order_id must be a valid UUID format

details.message: order_id must be a valid UUID format

422.description: Request validation failed before reaching application logic.

get.description: Return the public maker node identity, network, and current block height used by direct atomic-swap integrations. This endpoint is safe to poll.

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

post.description: Create a maker-side atomic swap from a fresh RFQ. The response contains the `swapstring` and `payment_hash` the taker must whitelist before execution. Treat this as non-idempotent: creating a second swap requires a new request.

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

400.description: The RFQ or swap parameters are invalid.

example.message: Invalid swap request: missing payment_hash or swapstring

422.description: Request validation failed before reaching application logic.

503.description: The trading service is still starting or temporarily unavailable.

example.message: Trading service is starting up. Please try again in a few moments.

post.description: Execute a previously initialized atomic swap after the taker has whitelisted the returned `swapstring`. Retry only when the previous attempt is known to have failed before execution was accepted.

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

400.description: The swapstring, taker pubkey, or payment hash is invalid.

example.message: Invalid swap request: missing payment_hash or swapstring

422.description: Request validation failed before reaching application logic.

503.description: The trading service is still starting or temporarily unavailable.

example.message: Trading service is starting up. Please try again in a few moments.

post.description: Fetch the current maker-side status for an atomic swap using its `payment_hash`. This endpoint is safe to poll repeatedly.

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

400.description: The supplied payment hash is invalid.

example.message: Invalid payment hash, please make sure that you are requesting with the correct payment hash.

404.description: No swap exists for the supplied payment hash.

422.description: Request validation failed before reaching application logic.

503.description: The trading service is still starting or temporarily unavailable.

example.message: Trading service is starting up. Please try again in a few moments.

get.description: List assets supported by the maker with optional filters and pagination. This endpoint is safe to poll and cache.

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

400.description: One of the supplied filters is invalid.

example.message: Invalid layer: BTC_FAKE. Valid layers: BTC_LN, BTC_L1, RGB_LN, RGB_L1, etc.

404.description: The requested asset could not be found.

422.description: Request validation failed before reaching application logic.

get.description: List supported trading pairs with filters for pair identifiers, asset tickers, layers, and pagination. This endpoint is safe to poll and cache.

parameters.name: layer

schema.description: Filter by layer (e.g., BTC_LN, RGB_LN, BTC_L1)
schema.title: Layer

parameters.description: Filter by layer (e.g., BTC_LN, RGB_LN, BTC_L1)

parameters.name: asset

schema.description: Filter by asset ticker or ID
schema.title: Asset

parameters.description: Filter by asset ticker or ID

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

example.detail: pair_ticker must be in format 'BASE/QUOTE', got: BTC-USDT

422.description: Request validation failed before reaching application logic.

post.description: Return the supported execution routes for a specific trading pair. Exactly one identification method must be provided.

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

400.description: The pair identifier payload is invalid.

example.detail: Exactly one identification method must be provided: pair_id, (from_asset_id + quote_asset_id), pair_ticker, or (base_ticker + quote_ticker)

404.description: No routes exist for the requested pair.

422.description: Request validation failed before reaching application logic.

post.description: Discover direct and multi-hop routes between assets. The response is informational and does not reserve liquidity.

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

422.description: Request validation failed before reaching application logic.

get.description: Return a matrix showing which assets can reach each other and the minimum hop count for each combination. Safe to poll and cache.

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

post.description: Request an RFQ-backed quote for a route between two asset legs. A successful response returns an `rfq_id` that can be consumed by swap-init or swap-order creation flows until the quote expires.

btc_ln_to_rgb_ln.summary: Quote BTC Lightning to RGB Lightning

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

400.description: The requested route or amount is invalid.

example.detail: Route not supported for this pair. From: BTC_LN, To: RGB_LN

404.description: The requested pair does not exist.

422.description: Request validation failed before reaching application logic.

503.description: The trading service is still starting or temporarily unavailable.

example.message: Trading service is starting up. Please try again in a few moments.

post.description: Create an order from a fresh RFQ for the web-style swap flow. The response returns a deposit destination and a per-order `access_token` that must be stored by the client for later status polling or rate decisions. Treat this operation as non-idempotent.

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

400.description: The quote, payout details, access token, or liquidity checks failed.

404.description: The requested trading pair or referenced resource does not exist.

422.description: Request validation failed before reaching application logic.

503.description: The trading service is still starting or temporarily unavailable.

example.message: Trading service is starting up. Please try again in a few moments.

post.description: Fetch the current state of a previously created swap order. Clients must send the `access_token` returned at order creation. This endpoint is safe to poll repeatedly.

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

400.description: The supplied access token is invalid.

404.description: No swap order exists for the supplied order ID.

422.description: Request validation failed before reaching application logic.

get.description: Return paginated historical swap orders. When no status filter is supplied the API defaults to filled orders.

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

get.description: Return aggregated swap-order counts by status plus filled-order volume metrics. Safe to poll and cache.

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

post.description: Resolve a pending swap-order repricing decision. The client must provide the per-order `access_token`. Accepting the new rate resumes execution with fresh pricing; declining requests a refund or manual review.

example.message: Refund requested. Manual review may still be required.

429.description: Too many requests. Respect the rate-limit headers before retrying.

400.description: The order is not awaiting a rate decision or the access token is invalid.

example.message: Order is not pending rate decision. Current state: FILLED

404.description: No swap order exists for the supplied order ID.

422.description: Request validation failed before reaching application logic.

example.message: Current market price unavailable. Please try again.

precision.title: Precision
precision.description: Decimal places (e.g., 8 for BTC, 6 for USDT)

Asset.title: Asset
Asset.description:
Extended asset definition for the trading system.

Extends TradableAsset with:
- Trading status (is_active)
- Supported layers (protocol/network combinations as strings)
- RGB-specific fields for node API compatibility
- Methods to convert to TradableAsset for specific settlements

Supported Layers Format: PROTOCOL/NETWORK
- BTC_L1, BTC_LN, BTC_SPARK, BTC_ARKADE, BTC_LIQUID, BTC_CASHU
- RGB_L1, RGB_LN
- TAPASS_L1, TAPASS_LN
- LIQUID_LIQUID, ARKADE_ARKADE, SPARK_SPARK

AssetDeliveryStatus.title: AssetDeliveryStatus
AssetDeliveryStatus.description: Status of asset delivery via keysend after channel opening

AssetsResponse.title: AssetsResponse
AssetsResponse.description: Response model for asset listing endpoints with pagination support.

from_asset.description: Complete input specification: asset, ticker, network, protocol, and amount

to_asset.description: Complete output specification: asset, ticker, network, protocol, and amount

receiver_address.description: Destination address/invoice for receiving the to_asset payout

request_id.title: Request Id
request_id.description: Request correlation identifier for support and debugging

Layer.title: Layer
Layer.description:
Settlement layer combining protocol and network as a single string.

Format: PROTOCOL/NETWORK

Each layer represents a valid combination where assets can exist and be transacted.
The layer encodes both WHAT protocol the asset uses and WHERE it settles.

total_hops.title: Total Hops
total_hops.description: Number of hops in the route

MultiHopRoute.title: MultiHopRoute
MultiHopRoute.description: Complete route with one or more steps.

OrderHistorySummary.title: OrderHistorySummary
OrderHistorySummary.description: Simplified order information for history listing

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of items matching the filter

has_next.title: Has Next
has_next.description: Whether there is a next page

has_previous.title: Has Previous
has_previous.description: Whether there is a previous page

PairQuoteRequest.title: PairQuoteRequest
PairQuoteRequest.description: Request for a quote on a trading pair using SwapLegInput.

from_asset.description: Complete source leg specification with amount and details

to_asset.description: Complete destination leg specification with amount and details

price.title: Price
price.description: Price of 1 whole unit of from_asset (e.g., 1 BTC) in terms of the smallest unit of to_asset (e.g., USDT with precision 6). Matches PriceData.price for the given rfq_id.

PairQuoteResponse.title: PairQuoteResponse
PairQuoteResponse.description:
Response containing a quote for a trading pair.

Uses full SwapLeg for both sides, providing complete asset details
including ticker, name, precision, layer, and amount.

payment_difference.title: Payment Difference
payment_difference.description: Payment difference in satoshis. Positive for overpayment, negative for underpayment, zero for exact payment

PaymentStatus.title: PaymentStatus
PaymentStatus.description: Payment status for onchain payments with support for partial payments.

RateDecisionRequest.title: RateDecisionRequest
RateDecisionRequest.description: Request for user to accept new rate or request refund

RateDecisionResponse.title: RateDecisionResponse
RateDecisionResponse.description: Response after user makes rate decision

example.message: Rate accepted. Order will proceed with current market rate.

address.title: Address
address.description: The actual address, invoice, or token string

ReceiverAddress.title: ReceiverAddress
ReceiverAddress.description:
Receiver address or invoice with its format.

Encapsulates the destination for receiving funds along with
metadata about what format it uses.

ReceiverAddressFormat.title: ReceiverAddressFormat
ReceiverAddressFormat.description:
Supported receiver address and invoice formats.

Different networks and protocols use different address/invoice formats
for receiving payments.

order_id.title: Order Id
order_id.description: Order ID to retry asset delivery for

RetryDeliveryRequest.title: RetryDeliveryRequest
RetryDeliveryRequest.description: Request model for /retry_delivery endpoint to trigger immediate keysend retry

example.message: Order queued for immediate asset-delivery retry.

indicative_price.title: Indicative Price
indicative_price.description: Indicative price from cached pair data

RouteStep.title: RouteStep
RouteStep.description: Single step in a route (one swap within a trading pair).

to_asset.title: To Asset
to_asset.description: Destination asset (if None, returns all reachable assets)

asset_id.title: Asset Id
asset_id.description: Unique identifier for the asset (e.g., 'BTC', RGB contract ID, etc.)

amount.title: Amount
amount.description: Amount of the asset in smallest unit. Must be greater than or equal to zero.

precision.title: Precision
precision.description: Number of decimal places for this asset (e.g., 8 for BTC, 6 or 8 for stablecoins)

SwapLeg.title: SwapLeg
SwapLeg.description:
Swap Leg: Complete asset specification for one side of a swap.

Represents a specific asset on a specific settlement layer with an amount.
This is the fundamental unit for swap orders - each side (leg) of a swap
specifies an asset on a particular network with a specific amount.

amount.title: Amount
amount.description: Amount in smallest unit (optional - one side must have amount)

SwapLegInput.title: SwapLegInput
SwapLegInput.description:
Lightweight input model for specifying one leg of a swap request.

Only contains the fields the client needs to provide. The service will
look up additional details (name, ticker, precision) from the asset registry.

rfq_id.title: Rfq Id
rfq_id.description: Quote identifier used to create the order

from_asset.description: Complete specification for input: asset, ticker, network, protocol, and amount

to_asset.description: Complete specification for output: asset, ticker, network, protocol, and amount

deposit_address.description: Address/Invoice for the user to deposit funds into

payout_address.description: Destination address/invoice for receiving the to_asset payout

filled_at.title: Filled At
filled_at.description: Timestamp when the order was filled

refund_txid.title: Refund Txid
refund_txid.description: Transaction ID of any refund sent

requires_manual_refund.title: Requires Manual Refund
requires_manual_refund.description: Whether the order requires manual refund handling

payment_difference.title: Payment Difference
payment_difference.description: Payment difference in satoshis (positive for overpayment, negative for underpayment)

last_payment_check.title: Last Payment Check
last_payment_check.description: Timestamp of the last payment verification

failure_reason.title: Failure Reason
failure_reason.description: Reason the order failed, if applicable

accept_new_rate.title: Accept New Rate
accept_new_rate.description: True to accept new rate, False to request refund

SwapOrderRateDecisionRequest.title: SwapOrderRateDecisionRequest
SwapOrderRateDecisionRequest.description: Request for user to accept new rate or request refund for a swap order

refund_txid.title: Refund Txid
refund_txid.description: Present if refund was requested and processed

SwapOrderRateDecisionResponse.title: SwapOrderRateDecisionResponse
SwapOrderRateDecisionResponse.description: Response after user makes rate decision for a swap order

example.message: Refund requested. Manual review may still be required.

SwapRoute.title: SwapRoute
SwapRoute.description:
Pre-computed valid swap route between two layers.

Represents a specific path for swapping: from_layer -> to_layer.
Frozen for immutability and hashability (enables use in sets, dict keys).

precision.title: Precision
precision.description: Decimal places (e.g., 8 for BTC, 6 for USDT)

TradableAsset.title: TradableAsset
TradableAsset.description:
Asset with layer context(s) for trading operations.

Supports both:
- Single-layer use cases (swap execution): use layer property
- Multi-layer use cases (trading pairs): use endpoints list

min_amount.title: Min Amount
min_amount.description: Minimum amount for trading (in smallest unit)

max_amount.title: Max Amount
max_amount.description: Maximum amount for trading (in smallest unit)

is_active.title: Is Active
is_active.description: Whether this layer is active for trading

TradingLimits.title: TradingLimits
TradingLimits.description:
Trading limits for a layer.

Combines a Layer (protocol/network) with min/max amount limits for trading.

TradingPair.title: TradingPair
TradingPair.description:
Complete trading pair with full asset details, routes, and price.

This is the unified model used for both:
- API responses (Pairs API)
- Database storage

Contains all pre-computed information needed to display and validate swaps.

TradingPairsResponse.title: TradingPairsResponse
TradingPairsResponse.description: Response containing list of trading pairs with pagination support.

tags.name: lsps1
tags.description: RGB LSPS1 liquidity endpoints for capability discovery, channel-order creation, fee estimation, order tracking, rate decisions, and asset delivery retries.

tags.name: market
tags.description: Public market discovery APIs for assets, trading pairs, routes, and RFQ-based quote requests.

tags.name: swaps
tags.description: Atomic swap APIs used by direct RLN integrations to initialize, execute, and track swaps.

tags.name: swap-orders
tags.description: Order-based swap APIs for web and service integrations that do not manage the atomic swap protocol directly.
